blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเข้าใจ Differences in Sex Development?
DSD = medical + ethical + psychosocial emergency. ต้องเปิด ambiguous genitalia ด้วย sensitivity และ urgency. 6 สิ่งสำคัญ: (1) CAH (21-OHD) = most common cause ของ ambiguous genitalia ใน newborn หญิง — salt-wasting crisis ฆ่าชีวิตได้ถ้ามีน Na/K ทุกวัน; (2) Testosterone + karyotype on Day 1 — levels fall rapidly, don't wait; (3) avoid sex assignment until workup complete; (4) multidisciplinary team required — ped endocrinology, urology, genetics, psychology; (5) gonadal malignancy risk ใน dysgenetic gonads สูง — gonadectomy timing เป็น decision ที่ต้องทำ; (6) genetic testing ยุคใหม่— cell-free DNA + DSD gene panels เปลี่ยน landscape ของ diagnosis.
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
อธิบาย normal sex development: chromosomal → gonadal → phenotypic sex — role ของ SRY, AMH, testosterone, DHT, 5α-reductase
จำแนก 4 กลุ่มใหญ่ของ DSD: 46,XX DSD / 46,XY DSD / chromosomal DSD / syndromes with incomplete genital development
วินิจฉัย CAH (21-OHD): 17-OHP, Prader staging, salt-wasting crisis management, sex assignment guidelines
แยก CAIS vs PAIS: karyotype, phenotype, androgen receptor, sex of rearing algorithm, gonadectomy timing
รู้ 5α-reductase type 2 deficiency: T → DHT pathway, phenotype at birth, virilization at puberty, sex assignment
อธิบาย testosterone biosynthetic defects: StAR, 3β-HSD, 17β-HSD type 3, Leydig cell hypoplasia — phenotype + adrenal involvement
รู้ complete vs partial 46,XY gonadal dysgenesis: gonadoblastoma risk, key genes (SRY, NR5A1, SOX8, NROB1, MAP3K1, DMRT1)
วินิจฉัย Turner syndrome, mixed gonadal dysgenesis, Denys-Drash: key features + workup
เลือก imaging ที่เหมาะสม: pelvic US / genitourethrogram / MRI — แต่ละตัวให้ข้อมูลอะไร
อธิบาย laboratory sequence: Day 1, Day 2–3, mini-puberty window, hCG test — timing + interpretation
รู้ hypospadias + cryptorchidism: when to evaluate for DSD, AUA guideline, orchiopexy timing
อธิบาย surgical management: clitoroplasty, vaginoplasty, gonadectomy — timing debates + outcomes data
1. Framework — 4 กลุ่มใหญ่ DSD และ Multidisciplinary Team
1.1 Classification ของ DSD
(1) 46,XX DSD: androgenization of 46,XX individual — CAH เป็นส่วนใหญ่ของ definable cases
(2) 46,XY DSD: incomplete androgenization of 46,XY — genetic diagnosis found in < 50% of cases
(3) Chromosomal/gonadal differentiation disorders: Turner (45,X), Klinefelter (47,XXY), mixed gonadal dysgenesis (45,X/46,XY), ovotesticular DSD
(4) Syndromes with incomplete genital development: Denys-Drash, WAGR, others — ดู Box 85.1 ใน textbook

หลักการสำคัญ: Avoid Premature Sex Assignment
Sex assignment ต้อง ไม่รีบ. ในอดีต sex assigned ตาม clitoral-phallic size — วิธีนี้ถูก criticized เพราะมีความไม่พอใจใน adults. Cultural + psychosocial factors สำคัญ. Sex assignment ต้องทำร่วมกัน multidisciplinary team + family.
1.2 Multidisciplinary Team ที่ต้องการ
Core team: primary care physician, neonatologist, ped endocrinologist, psychologist, ped urologist, ped geneticist, ped radiologist
เพิ่มเติม: adolescent gynecologist, cytogeneticist, ethicist — แล้วแต่ case
Psychological support ของ family เริ่มใน newborn period + long-term follow-up essential
2. Normal Sex Development — Chromosomal → Gonadal → Phenotypic
2.1 Chromosomal Sex
Determined at conception: sperm carrying X → 46,XX (female); sperm carrying Y → 46,XY (male)
SRY (sex-determining region of Y chromosome): necessary but NOT sufficient for testicular differentiation; only Y chromosome gene involved in testicular determination
SRY → instructs medullary region of bipotential gonad → Sertoli cells → testicular cords → seminiferous tubules; expressed at low levels in Sertoli cells until adulthood
2.2 Key Genes in Sex Differentiation

rows: Mutation -> complete 46,XY gonadal dysgenesis (15% of cases)
Master regulator: SRY, SOX9, AMH, LHCGR, StAR, CYP11A1
46,XY: range from complete GD to infertility; ovotesticular DSD
Upregulated by SRY; Sertoli cell differentiation
Mutations: campomelic dysplasia + testicular dysgenesis 75% of XY
Haploinsufficiency -> 46,XY gonadal dysgenesis; deletion -> female development in XY
Duplication -> 46,XY female (gonadal dysgenesis); loss-of-function -> adrenal hypoplasia congenita
Critical for ovarian + female sexual development
Mutation -> mullerian duct regression + virilization in 46,XX; duplication -> 46,XY female
Synergizes with NR5A1 to activate AMH promoter
Heterozygous changes -> congenital heart disease + 46,XY DSD

blocks.text: 2.3 Gonadal Sex — Timeline
Germ cells migrate from yolk sac to genital ridge 4–6 weeks' gestation; somatic cells → Sertoli cells (testis) / granulosa cells (ovary)
Gonadal sex established 7 weeks' gestation; fetus contains 2 parallel duct systems: wolffian (mesonephric) + mullerian (paramesonephric)

If testis develops: Sertoli cells secrete AMH (anti-mullerian hormone) by 8 weeks → mullerian duct regression (exocrine, unilateral); Leydig cells differentiate 9–10 weeks → secrete testosterone → wolffian ducts → epididymis, vas deferens, seminal vesicles
If ovary develops: no AMH → mullerian ducts → proximal vagina, uterus, fallopian tubes; no testosterone → wolffian ducts involute (ยกเว้น Gartner duct remnant)
2.4 Phenotypic Sex — External Genitalia
Androgenization of external genitalia starts 8 weeks' gestation; nearly complete by 12–14 weeks
Mechanism: testosterone → 5α-reductase type 2 (in target cells) → DHT (dihydrotestosterone) → androgenization via intact androgen receptor
Testosterone production: driven by maternal HCG (first trimester) → fetal LH (16–20 weeks)
Testicular descent: Phase 1 transabdominal (8–15 weeks, INSL3 + GREAT receptor); Phase 2 inguinoscrotal (25–35 weeks, androgen-dependent) → ผู้คลอดก่อนกำหนด มี cryptorchidism สูง

AMH Actions — Unilateral + Local (NOT Systemic)
AMH ออกฤทธิ์แบบ unilateral exocrine — ต้องผลิตได้อย่างเพียงพอ ไม่ใช่ bilateral systemic. Remnants of mullerian ducts in males: prostatic utricle (caudal) + appendix testis (cephalic). Elevated serum testosterone ใน 46,XX CAH — ไม่ทำให้ wolffian duct stabilize เพราะไม่มี local exposure.
3. Clinical Assessment of DSD
3.1 History

Family history: early neonatal deaths, consanguinity, urogenital anomalies, female infertility, amenorrhea, mullerian duct abnormalities, ambiguous genitalia in male maternal relatives (X-linked → CAIS)
Maternal androgenization: hirsutism, severe acne, deepening voice, clitoromegaly — บ่งบอก androgen excess affecting fetus
Medications: cimetidine, spironolactone, hydantoin, progestational agents — ทุกตัวส่งผลต่อ genital development; progesterone IUD เพิ่ม risk; ART ใช้ progesterone support first trimester
3.2 Physical Examination

Degree of androgenization: Prader staging I–V (females with CAH); External Masculinization Score (EMS): 4 parameters (micropenis, urethral meatus, labioscrotal fusion, gonad position) — normal male = 12
Palpable gonads: ถ้า gonad palpable → มักเป็น testis หรือ ovotestis; absent palpable gonads + ambiguous genitalia = CAH จนกว่าจะพิสูจน์ไม่ใช่
Separate urethral + vaginal openings vs single perineal opening (urogenital sinus); low vs high vaginal entry — surgical implications
Dysmorphic features: Turner syndrome → gonadal dysgenesis; multiple anomalies → syndromes (Denys-Drash, WAGR)
Penis stretch length reference: Feldman & Smith J Pediatr 1975: 28–40 wks GA — mean ± 2 SD ให้ cutoff สำหรับ microphallus
3.3 Radiologic Investigations

rows: Presence/absence of uterus (CRITICAL), adrenal size, gonad location
Sensitivity 45%, specificity 78% for testis location; normal adrenal size does NOT rule out CAH
Urethral configuration, vaginal presence, level of vaginal entry into UGS, prostatic utricle
Fluoroscopic dye study; experienced radiologist needed; examine ALL perineal orifices
Mullerian/wolffian structures, gonad position, soft tissue characterization; differentiates clitoris from penis (bulbospongiosus absent in virilized female)
Expensive; may require anesthesia; highest sensitivity/specificity; anesthesia risk in neonate

blocks.text:
Uterus Present or Absent — Critical First Step
Presence of well-developed uterus on US → เจเจาะ 46,XX DSD (CAH/virilization), 46,XY ovotesticular DSD, 46,XY complete gonadal dysgenesis, PMDS. No uterus + ambiguous genitalia → นึกถึง 46,XY DSD (AMH normal → mullerian regression occurred). ข้อควรระวัง: inability to identify uterus on US ไม่ใช่ conclusive proof of absence. MRI อาจจำเป็น.
3.4 Laboratory Investigations — Timeline

Day 1 of life: serum testosterone + DHT by LC-MS/MS (other assays measure interfering substances → falsely elevated T); karyotype + FISH for SRY; 17-OHP elevated physiologically day 1 — don't use for CAH screening on Day 1
Day 2–3: 17-OHP, 17-hydroxypregnenolone, 11-deoxycortisol, DHEA, androstenedione, plasma renin activity (CAH suspected); ACTH stimulation ถ้า ACTH level ยังไม่ significantly elevated
After 1 week: LH + FSH (suppressed immediate newborn period); useful for AIS, gonadal dysgenesis, LH receptor abnormalities
Mini-puberty window (2–8 weeks): repeat LH, FSH, T, DHT — physiologic testosterone surge in healthy male infants; ใช้ประเมิน incomplete androgenization in 46,XY males
AMH + inhibin B: anytime — AMH very low in anorchia; intermediately low with abnormal testes; helpful for gonadal presence evaluation

hCG stimulation test: testosterone response > 200 ng/dL = normal (rules out T biosynthetic defect); T:DHT < 8:1 = normal (rules out 5α-reductase deficiency; caveat: gold standard is genetic testing); hCG ยัง → phallic enlargement (0.25–0.75 cm/5 days)
Salt-wasting vigilance: Na + K daily ถ้าสงสัย CAH; elevated renin + lower Na = impending crisis → เริ่ม hydrocortisone + fludrocortisone + NaCl supplementation
4. 46,XX DSD — Androgenization of Genetic Female
4.1 Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia — 21-Hydroxylase Deficiency (21-OHD)
Most common cause of ambiguous genitalia in newborn female; population frequency 1:15,000; autosomal recessive; males + females equally affected genetically

Mechanism: 21-hydroxylase deficiency → excess 17-OHP → shunted to androgen pathway → androstenedione → testosterone or DHT via backdoor pathway (Miller & Auchus PLoS Biol 2019) → androgenization of 46,XX external genitalia
Internal genitalia (ovaries, uterus, fallopian tubes) = normal; no wolffian structures (no local testosterone); male with 21-OHD = phenotypically normal
Prader staging:
Stage I: mild clitoromegaly only
Stage II: clitoromegaly + labial fusion
Stage III: intermediate — clitoromegaly + labioscrotal fusion + single perineal opening
Stage IV: severe virilization, phallus-like clitoris + scrotal-like labia
Stage V: complete masculinization — urethral opening at tip of phallus; phenotypically male
Diagnosis: 17-OHP after Day 1 — typically 50–100× normal (≥10,000 ng/dL) in classic cases; plasma renin ↑ + low Na = salt-wasting

Sex assignment: female for 46,XX CAH (traditional, current standard); gender identity typically female; prenatal androgen exposure affects behavior but NOT gender identity (aged 5–12 yr); gender reassignment F→M reported in profoundly virilized cases but female assignment prevails
Fertility: 46,XX females with CAH are potentially fertile (ovaries + uterus intact)
4.2 Other 46,XX DSD
11β-hydroxylase deficiency: excess 11-deoxycortisol + DOC → virilization + hypertension; less common than 21-OHD

POR (P450 oxidoreductase) deficiency: similar to 17,20-lyase deficiency; XX infants → ambiguous genitalia + skeletal defects (Antley-Bixler syndrome); low maternal estriol; maternal virilization may or may not occur
Maternal virilizing disorder: medications (progestins), adrenal/ovarian tumors, luteoma of pregnancy, aromatase deficiency → maternal + fetal virilization
46,XX ovotesticular DSD: ovotestis on US/biopsy — ตรวจพบอนุพันธุ์ทั้ง ovarian follicles + testicular tubules; NR5A1 p.Arg92Trp mutation in ~10% of cases; breast development common, menses up to 50%

46,XX testicular DSD: SRY translocated from Y to X chromosome; 90% normal male external genitalia, 10% hypospadias; usually diagnosed at puberty (small testes + gynecomastia) or as adult (infertility); unlike Klinefelter, NOT tall (lacks extra SHOX gene copies)
5. 46,XY DSD — Incomplete Androgenization
5.1 Complete Androgen Insensitivity Syndrome (CAIS)
AR gene mutations (Xq) — >800 germline mutations; no genotype-phenotype correlation; X-linked recessive

Phenotype: completely female external genitalia in 46,XY; vagina short, blind-ending; no mullerian structures (testes produce AMH → mullerian regression); no wolffian structures; testes: abdomen/inguinal canal/labia majora
Presentation: inguinal hernia เป็น presenting sign ใน prepubertal girls; primary amenorrhea + lack of pubic/axillary hair after puberty; robust breast development (peripheral aromatization T → E2)
Mini-puberty: absent testosterone production during mini-puberty (no androgen receptor function); LH elevated; no spermatogenesis
Cell-free DNA testing: increasingly diagnoses at birth (discordance between cfDNA showing XY + female phenotype)

Gonadectomy: germ cell tumor risk < 1% before puberty and during puberty → conservative management until puberty is recommended; no consensus on orchidectomy timing; alternatives: orchiopexy + surveillance. Shared decision-making with adolescent patient critical
5.2 Partial Androgen Insensitivity Syndrome (PAIS)
Also AR mutations; various degrees of external genital ambiguity; Reifenstein syndrome = historical term for PAIS with intermediate androgenization
Physical findings: small phallus + ventral chordee + penoscrotal hypospadias + bifid scrotum ± gonads; cryptorchidism common; no mullerian structures; wolffian structures absent or poorly developed

Diagnosis complex: AR sequencing detects up to 95% of CAIS/PAIS mutations; AR binding assay ปกติไม่ rule out AIS; postzygotic mutations → mosaicism; differentiate from 17β-HSD deficiency type 3 (พลาด → virilizes at puberty)

Sex of rearing: Prader IV–V → male assignment; Prader I–II → female; Prader III → testosterone responsiveness helps; gender identity dysphoria in 12% of female-assigned and 25% of male-assigned PAIS (not statistically significant, Babu J Pediatr Urol 2021)
5.3 5α-Reductase Type 2 Deficiency
Autosomal recessive: inability to convert T → DHT — DHT required for male external genital development

Wide phenotype at birth: small phallus appearing as normal/slightly enlarged clitoris → severe penoscrotal hypospadias + bifid scrotum; testes usually inguinal or in labioscrotal folds; wolffian structures present (T normal); no mullerian structures (AMH normal)

At puberty: virilization common (other 5α-reductase isoenzymes active) — phallic enlargement 4–6 cm; frequent reports of female-to-male gender behavior reversal after puberty; accumulated evidence supports male sex assignment (Wilson Endocr Rev 1993)
Diagnosis: gold standard = genetic testing (SRD5A2 gene); urinary 5α/5β steroid ratios; T:DHT ratio > 8:1 (caveat: misleading if T not high enough)
5.4 Testosterone Biosynthetic Defects

6 enzymes จำเป็นสำหรับ testosterone synthesis. Defects ใน first 4 enzymes → both underandrogenized male AND CAH (ประสิทธิ adrenal + gonadal). Wolffian structures underdeveloped; mullerian absent (AMH normal).

rows: StAR protein / Congenital lipoid adrenal hyperplasia
Lipid accumulation adrenal+gonad; ACTH+renin up, all steroids low
Low androgens + low estrogens; hypertension in XY + XX
Ambiguous or feminine; mild virilization in XX females
Female at birth; virilizes at puberty
Elevated androstenedione, low-normal T, high LH
Female to ambiguous; no pubertal virilization
High LH, low T; no testosterone response to hCG

blocks.text:
17β-HSD Type 3: คนไข้หญิง → วิริลไดเจน์ที่ยิ่งสำคัญ
17β-HSD type 3 deficiency: ทารกจะ ดูเป็นเพศหญิงที่แรกเกิด — ถ้าไม่วินิจฉัย จะ raise as female. แต่ไม่ใช่ PAIS! At puberty: progressive virilization (other 17β-HSD isoenzymes convert androstenedione → T) — phallus 4–6 cm, testes at inguinal canal, male body habitus, deepening voice. Misdiagnose as PAIS = serious error: prognosis + sex assignment implications ต่างกัน.
6. Gonadal Dysgenesis (46,XY)
6.1 Complete 46,XY Gonadal Dysgenesis (Swyer Syndrome)
Phenotype: completely female external genitalia in 46,XY; mullerian structures present (streak gonads produce no AMH); no wolffian structures; streak gonads intra-abdominal
Tumor risk: gonadoblastoma HIGH risk in dysgenetic gonads → prophylactic gonadectomy indicated (Looijenga Best Pract Res Clin Endocrinol Metab 2007)
Management: gonadectomy early → estrogen replacement for feminization + menses with progesterone; can carry pregnancy with donor oocytes
6.2 Partial 46,XY Gonadal Dysgenesis
Ambiguous genitalia at birth; degree of androgenization depends on extent of testicular differentiation; gonadal tissue usually intra-abdominal
T response to hCG variable/low; mix of mullerian + wolffian structures; biopsy: poorly formed disorganized seminiferous tubules + wavy ovarian stroma
6.3 Key Genes in 46,XY Gonadal Dysgenesis

rows: Mutations: ~15% of complete 46,XY GD; HMG domain mutations; variable penetrance (father can carry same mutation)
Master regulator; range: complete GD -> infertility; p.Arg92Trp acts as molecular switch (Bashamboo Hum Mol Genet 2016)
Heterozygous missense -> complete 46,XY GD (shares HMG motif with SRY)
Duplication -> complete 46,XY GD; loss-of-function -> adrenal hypoplasia congenita + cryptorchidism + HH at puberty
Heterozygous mutations -> severe hypospadias; also seen in other 46,XY DSD with hypospadias
Mutations in early testis cords/Sertoli cells; range: complete GD to isolated hypospadias to micropenis + cryptorchidism
Heterozygous -> congenital heart disease + 46,XY DSD; interacts with SRY, SOX9, AMH
Haploinsufficiency DMRT1 -> gonadal dysgenesis; 9p24.3 deletion range: GD, ovotestis, hypospadias, penoscrotal transposition, cryptorchidism

blocks.text: 6.4 Testicular Regression Syndrome
46,XY; prior testicular function then regression (loss 8–10 weeks → ambiguous genitalia + variable internal structures; loss after 12–14 weeks → normal male genitalia + small phallus)
Vanishing testis syndrome: testes lost second half pregnancy (torsion/vascular anomaly); normal external + internal ducts; FSH elevated in infancy; AMH very low in anorchia
7. Chromosomal/Gonadal Disorders
7.1 Turner Syndrome (45,X)
Incidence 1/2000 female births; premature ovarian failure can occur before birth
Cardiac: bicuspid aortic valve, coarctation of aorta, anomalous pulmonary veins — screen with echocardiogram
Renal: horseshoe kidney — renal ultrasound recommended
Audiologic evaluation: formal audiology; ophthalmologic exam at 1 year (strabismus risk); hip dysplasia screening
Endocrine: short stature + premature ovarian failure + hypothyroidism risk; long-term endocrinology follow-up; yearly thyroid function tests
Neurodevelopment: normal intelligence; decreased visual-spatial skills, information processing, math skills, social skills (Hong Dev Disabil Res Rev 2009)
Cell-free DNA prenatal testing: increasingly diagnosed prenatally (Badeau Cochrane 2017)
7.2 Mixed Gonadal Dysgenesis (45,X/46,XY)
Asymmetric gonadal dysgenesis + ambiguous genitalia; most common karyotype 45,X/46,XY; wide phenotypic spectrum
Classic: incomplete uterus present in almost all; fallopian tubes always on streak gonad side; wolffian structures on dysgenetic gonad side
Tumor risk: streak gonads must be removed — high gonadoblastoma risk; sex assignment guided by degree of androgenization
Prenatal diagnosis 45,X/46,XY: most prenatally-diagnosed cases → normal male phenotype (Davidoff Pediatrics 1973; Wheeler Am J Med Genet 1988); may have Turner features (short stature, cardiac)
7.3 Klinefelter Syndrome (47,XXY)
Tall stature (3 SHOX gene copies); hypogonadotropic hypogonadism at puberty; small testes; infertility; gynecomastia; diagnosed increasingly by prenatal cfDNA
Management: testosterone replacement at puberty; fertility preservation options (sperm extraction); EAA guidelines (Zitzmann Andrology 2021)
8. Syndromes Associated with Ambiguous Genitalia
8.1 Denys-Drash Syndrome
Classic triad: 46,XY ambiguous/female genitalia + congenital nephrotic syndrome (mesangial sclerosis → end-stage renal failure) + Wilms tumor
WT1 gene mutations: >90% of cases; WT1 critical for normal genital tract development; also associated with WAGR (Wilms tumor, aniridia, genitourinary anomalies, mental retardation)
Renal biopsy: mesangial sclerosis; proteinuria noted at early age; renal failure common — screen all DSD patients for WT1-related renal disease
8.2 Other Syndromes with Genital Differences
WAGR: Wilms tumor + aniridia + genitourinary anomalies + mental retardation; WT1 deletions at 11p13
Aarskog syndrome: short forearms (100% of cases), macrocephaly, hypertelorism, small upturned nose; undescended testes 65% of males; hypospadias may be severe; cognitive performance usually normal
9. Other Disorders of Genital Differentiation
9.1 Hypospadias
Incidence: 4–8 per 1000 male births; glanular + subcoronal > 50% of all hypospadias
Classification: glanular, subcoronal, distal penile, midshaft, proximal penile, penoscrotal, scrotal, perineal

DSD association: severe (scrotal/penoscrotal) hypospadias → 36% had underlying DSD in Albers study (33 patients: Denys-Drash 3, PAIS 2, ovotesticular DSD 2, chromosomal 1, AMH abnormality 1, gonadal dysgenesis 1, 5α-reductase 1, 46,XX SRY+ 1); undescended testes in 11/12
Evaluation: severe hypospadias → karyotype + T biosynthesis evaluation required, especially if + undescended testes

Associated factors: Aarskog ~15% prevalence of DSD with hypospadias; IUGR + prematurity (placental insufficiency → lower hCG → lower androgen early pregnancy); endocrine disruptors; genes AKR1C2/C3/C4
9.2 Cryptorchidism
Incidence: 15–30% premature; 1–3% term; 2-phase descent: transabdominal (first trimester, INSL3) + inguinoscrotal (25–30 weeks, androgen-dependent)
Spontaneous descent: most premature infants with cryptorchidism will descend by 6 months of corrected age
Hormonal treatment: not recommended — Cortes J Urol 2000: hormonal treatment may harm germ cells in 1–3-year-old boys with cryptorchidism

Surgery: orchiopexy preferred; AUA Guideline (Kolon J Urol 2014): evaluation + treatment of cryptorchidism; US ผล sensitivity 45%, specificity 78% — not recommended by AUA for locating non-palpable testis
10. NICU Practical — Ambiguous Genitalia Call

Day 1 actions: (1) notify multidisciplinary team; (2) do NOT assign sex prematurely; (3) testosterone + DHT by LC-MS/MS — ทำให้เร็วที่สุด, falls rapidly; (4) karyotype + FISH for SRY; (5) pelvic US — uterus present or not
Salt-wasting vigilance: ถ้ามี absent palpable gonads + ambiguous genitalia = CAH จนกว่าจะพิสูจน์ไม่ใช่. Check Na + K daily; 17-OHP on Day 2–3; renin + electrolytes if crisis impending
Parent communication: open + honest; avoid premature sex announcement; frame as 'your baby's genitals are not fully formed yet and we're working to understand why'; psychological support from Day 1
Imaging sequence: pelvic US first (uterus present/absent); genitourethrogram if urogenital sinus suspected; MRI if US inconclusive and decision-making requires soft tissue detail

CAIS detected by cfDNA discordance: female phenotype + XY karyotype on cfDNA → pelvic US (no uterus, no ovaries) → confirm karyotype → AR sequencing; อธิบายครอบครัว carefully — gender identity female; gonadectomy discussion deferred to adolescence

Gonadal malignancy — who needs early gonadectomy: (1) complete 46,XY gonadal dysgenesis (high risk gonadoblastoma); (2) mixed gonadal dysgenesis streak gonads; (3) dysplastic testis in XY male. CAIS: conservative — < 1% before puberty
46,XX CAH: hydrocortisone (physiologic dose) + fludrocortisone + NaCl supplementation; clitoral recession surgery ถ้าไว้ (debate: delay vs early) — nerve-sparing ventral clitoroplasty preferred
11. Surgical Management Overview
11.1 Clitoroplasty
Indication: severe clitoral enlargement in 46,XX CAH
Modern approach: nerve-sparing ventral clitoroplasty; preserve dorsal neurovascular bundle + glans for better sensitivity (Kogan 1983; Snyder); clitoral erectile tissue reduced, glans preserved

Controversy: clitoral atrophy or excessive regrowth in adolescent/adult outcome studies; many advocate delay until puberty when patient can participate in decision; emotional trauma of adolescent surgery vs technical ease in early childhood
11.2 Vaginoplasty
Reconstruction of vagina in virilized females: critical decision = level of confluence of vagina + urethra in urogenital sinus
Low confluence (distal to external sphincter): flap vaginoplasty or cutback procedure
High confluence (proximal to external sphincter): pull-through vaginoplasty or total urogenital mobilization (Hendren & Crawford technique)

Timing debate: prepubertal vaginoplasty → risk vaginal stenosis (multiple studies); many authors delay vaginoplasty to puberty or later when estrogenization of vaginal mucosa prevents stricture and patient can perform manual dilation
11.3 Gonadectomy

Indications for pre-consent gonadectomy: assigned sex different from gonadal sex + HIGH tumor risk; mainly 46,XY complete gonadal dysgenesis (gonadoblastoma risk at YOUNG age); mixed gonadal dysgenesis streak gonads
CAIS: conservative management until puberty (tumor risk < 1% in childhood); alternatives: orchiopexy + imaging surveillance + biopsy; share decision-making with adolescent
DSD with virilization risk at puberty: hormonal suppression (GnRH analog) + sex hormone replacement congruent with gender identity = alternative to surgery until patient can consent
11.4 Removal of Mullerian Remnants
NOT recommended unless symptomatic (dysuria, UTI, pain, bleeding, herniation, stone); laparoscopic removal if indicated; malignancy rare
12. 5 Bedside Pearls

PEARL 1 — No Palpable Gonads + Ambiguous Genitalia = CAH Until Proven Otherwise
ถ้า newborn มี ambiguous genitalia + ไม่คลำพบ gonads เลย → ให้นึกถึง CAH (46,XX) ทันที. ตรวจ Na, K ทุกวัน; อย่าเอา 17-OHP ในวันแรกของชีวิต (ทาง physiologic สูง). Salt-wasting crisis ฆ่า life-threatening ได้เร็ว — elevated renin + low Na = emergency. เริ่ม hydrocortisone + fludrocortisone + NaCl ทันที.

PEARL 2 — Testosterone on Day 1 Only — It Falls Fast
Testosterone ลดลงอย่างรวดเร็วในโสตสามวันแรกของชีวิต — Day 1 = ideal time to collect. Use LC-MS/MS assay เท่านั้น — assays อื่น measure interfering substances → falsely elevated T ทำให้ผล misleading. มี mini-puberty surge 2–8 weeks — ใช้ window นี้เพื่อตรวจ incomplete androgenization ใน 46,XY infant ถ้าวันแรกพลาด.

PEARL 3 — CAIS: Female Baby + XY karyotype + No Uterus = Explain Carefully
CAIS เป็น completely female phenotype. cfDNA prenatal testing เพิ่ม diagnosis ใน newborn period. ครอบครัวต้องการ time และ support. Gender identity = female; raise as female. Gonadectomy ยังไม่ต้องทำด่วน (tumor risk < 1% before puberty); defer gonadectomy decision to adolescent with shared decision-making. Estrogen replacement at puberty needed after gonadectomy.

PEARL 4 — Complete 46,XY Gonadal Dysgenesis → Gonadectomy Early
Swyer syndrome: female phenotype + 46,XY + streak gonads + mullerian structures present. Gonadoblastoma risk HIGH in dysgenetic gonads — ต่างจาก CAIS อย่างมาก. Prophylactic gonadectomy required early (Looijenga Best Pract Res Clin Endocrinol Metab 2007). หลัง gonadectomy: estrogen replacement + progesterone → menses. Pregnancy possible with donor oocytes.

PEARL 5 — 5α-Reductase Deficiency: Female at Birth, Male at Puberty
Raised female → virilizes dramatically at puberty: phallic enlargement, male voice, mustache. Accumulated evidence: male sex assignment (Wilson Endocr Rev 1993); but if raised female + female gender identity → GnRH analog + estrogen. Diagnosis: SRD5A2 gene testing (gold standard; T:DHT ratio > 8:1 can be misleading). อย่า confuse กับ 17β-HSD type 3 — ทั้งสองตัวเป็น female at birth and virilize at puberty; differentiate by androstenedione levels + genetic testing.
13. Evidence Update — Landmark Studies

Hughes IA, et al. Best Pract Res Clin Endocrinol Metab 2007;21:351 (ESPE/LWPES guidelines): Consensus guidelines for diagnosis and treatment of DSD; multidisciplinary team mandatory; avoid early sex assignment; long-term psychological outcome data needed.
Ahmed SF, Rodie M. Best Pract Res Clin Endocrinol Metab 2010;24:197: Investigation and initial management of ambiguous genitalia — step-by-step clinical algorithm for newborn evaluation.

Miller WL, Auchus RJ. PLoS Biol 2019;17(4):e3000198: The 'backdoor pathway' of androgen synthesis in human male sexual development — androstenedione → DHT via backdoor in CAH; explains degree of virilization.
Bashamboo A, McElreavey K. Sex Dev 2016;10:313: Mechanism of sex determination in humans: insights from DSD — comprehensive review of sex determination genes and how mutations cause DSD.

Bashamboo A, et al. Hum Mol Genet 2016;25(23):5286 (NR5A1 p.Arg92Trp): Recurrent p.Arg92Trp variant in NR5A1 acts as molecular switch in human sex development; found in ~10% of ovotesticular DSD cases.

Looijenga LH, et al. Best Pract Res Clin Endocrinol Metab 2007;21:431: Tumor risk in DSD — risk stratification for gonadoblastoma; complete GD and mixed GD streak gonads = high risk; CAIS = low risk before puberty.

Canalichio KL, et al. Endocrine 2020;70:170: Non-surgical approach to 46,XY DSD — GnRH analog + sex hormone replacement to suppress virilization at puberty while preserving patient decision-making about surgery.

Kolon TF, et al. J Urol 2014;192:337 (AUA guideline): Evaluation and treatment of cryptorchidism — US not recommended for non-palpable testis localization; orchiopexy preferred; hormonal treatment discouraged (Cortes 2000).

Gravholt CH, et al. Eur J Endocrinol 2017;177:G1 (Turner syndrome CPG): Clinical practice guidelines for care of girls and women with Turner syndrome: cardiac, renal, audiologic, ophthalmologic, endocrine screening recommendations.
Wilson JD. Endocr Rev 1993;14(5):577: Steroid 5alpha-reductase 2 deficiency — foundational review; accumulated evidence supports male sex assignment; virilization at puberty common.

Mendonca BB, et al. Medicine 2000;79(5):299: 17beta-HSD deficiency — gender role changes female to male in 3/10 despite androgenization; female gender role maintained in others; sex of rearing culturally influenced.
Moshiri M, et al. Radiographics 2012;32:1599: Evaluation and management of DSD: multidisciplinary approach — imaging role (US, genitourethrogram, MRI); clinical-radiologic correlations.

Babu R, Shah U. J Pediatr Urol 2021;17(1):39: Gender identity disorder in adolescents/adults with DSD: systematic review and meta-analysis — gender identity dysphoria 12% female-assigned PAIS and 25% male-assigned PAIS (not statistically significant).
New MI. Semin Perinatol 1992;16(5):299: Female pseudohermaphroditism — classic review of 46,XX DSD; CAH most common cause; Prader staging; 17-OHP diagnostic criteria.

Badeau M, et al. Cochrane Database Syst Rev 2017;11:CD011767: Genomics-based non-invasive prenatal testing for fetal chromosomal aneuploidy — cfDNA testing accuracy for 45,X, 47,XXY, and sex chromosome aneuploidies.
14. Self-Check Questions พร้อมเฉลย
Q: Newborn female-appearing infant born with karyotype discordance (cfDNA: XY) — US shows no uterus, no ovaries. Diagnosis? Next steps?

ตอบ: Diagnosis: Complete Androgen Insensitivity Syndrome (CAIS) จนกว่าจะพิสูจน์ไม่ใช่. Next steps: confirm karyotype (blood), AR gene sequencing, testosterone (will be elevated), AMH (elevated, testes producing). Raise as female — gender identity female. Gonadectomy: NOT urgent — germ cell tumor risk < 1% before puberty; defer to adolescent shared decision-making. Estrogen replacement needed post-gonadectomy for feminization + bone health. Family counseling: open + honest discussion about XY karyotype, normal female identity.
Q: Newborn with ambiguous genitalia, no palpable gonads, Day 2 — 17-OHP = 15,000 ng/dL. What is the diagnosis? What do you watch for and how do you treat?

ตอบ: Diagnosis: Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia — 21-hydroxylase deficiency (46,XX DSD). 17-OHP 50–100× normal = classic CAH. Watch for salt-wasting crisis: check Na + K daily; plasma renin activity; aldosterone. Elevated renin + low Na = impending crisis. Treatment: hydrocortisone (10–15 mg/m²/day divided) + fludrocortisone (0.05–0.2 mg/day) + NaCl supplementation. Sex assignment: female (traditional). Clitoroplasty: controversial timing; nerve-sparing preferred.
Q: 46,XY infant with female-appearing genitalia, mullerian structures present on US. No salt-wasting. Testosterone undetectable. What is the diagnosis?

ตอบ: Diagnosis: Complete 46,XY Gonadal Dysgenesis (Swyer syndrome). Streak gonads produce no testosterone (female genitalia) + no AMH (mullerian structures present). Differentiate from CAIS (CAIS: no mullerian structures, T elevated, AMH elevated). Gonadoblastoma risk HIGH in streak gonads → prophylactic gonadectomy indicated (Looijenga 2007). Estrogen replacement after gonadectomy. Pregnancy possible with donor oocytes. Genetic testing: SRY, NR5A1, SOX8, DMRT1.
Q: 46,XY infant with ambiguous genitalia, wolffian duct structures present, no mullerian structures. T:DHT ratio = 15:1 after hCG. What is the most likely diagnosis?

ตอบ: T:DHT ratio > 8:1 = 5α-reductase type 2 deficiency (cannot convert T → DHT; DHT required for external genital androgenization). Wolffian present (testosterone normal) + no mullerian (AMH normal) = testes functional. Caveat: T:DHT can mislead if T not adequately high; gold standard = SRD5A2 gene testing. Sex assignment: male recommended (accumulated evidence, Wilson Endocr Rev 1993); pubertal virilization likely (other 5α-reductase isoenzymes). If female gender identity at puberty: GnRH analog + estrogen.
Q: แยก complete 46,XY gonadal dysgenesis จาก CAIS อย่างไร? 3 ความแตกต่างสำคัญ?

ตอบ: (1) Mullerian structures: Swyer = present (streak gonads produce NO AMH); CAIS = absent (testes produce AMH normally). (2) Testosterone level: Swyer = undetectable/very low (no functional testes); CAIS = elevated (testes produce T but receptor nonfunctional). (3) Gonadoblastoma risk: Swyer = HIGH → early gonadectomy; CAIS = LOW (< 1% before puberty) → conservative. Both: female phenotype, 46,XY karyotype, raised female.
Q: Denys-Drash syndrome: classic triad คืออะไร? gene? และทำไม neonatologist ต้องรู้?

ตอบ: Classic triad: 46,XY ambiguous/female genitalia + congenital nephrotic syndrome (mesangial sclerosis) + Wilms tumor. Gene: WT1 mutations > 90% of cases; WT1 also associated with WAGR. Neonatologist ต้องรู้: infant with 46,XY DSD + proteinuria/renal disease = Denys-Drash จนกว่าจะพิสูจน์ไม่ใช่. Renal biopsy: mesangial sclerosis. Wilms tumor screening by US. WT1 sequencing.
Q: Severe (penoscrotal) hypospadias in a 46,XY newborn with bilateral undescended testes — what workup is indicated?

ตอบ: Severe hypospadias + undescended testes = DSD found in ~36% (Albers study, J Pediatr 1997). Required workup: (1) karyotype; (2) testosterone biosynthesis evaluation — T, DHT, androstenedione, 17-OHP on Day 2–3; (3) pelvic US; (4) genitourethrogram; (5) genetic panel (NR5A1, AR, SRD5A2, HSD17B3). Multidisciplinary team referral. AUA Guideline (Kolon J Urol 2014): evaluate for DSD if severe hypospadias + undescended testis.
Q: 46,XY infant with bilateral undescended testes, elevated LH, no testosterone response to hCG — diagnosis?

ตอบ: Diagnosis: Leydig cell hypoplasia (LH receptor mutation). LH receptor mutation → testes cannot respond to LH or hCG → no testosterone production. High LH + low T + no hCG response (< 200 ng/dL). No adrenal involvement (unlike StAR or 17α-hydroxylase deficiency). Phenotype: female to ambiguous; no pubertal virilization. Differentiate from StAR deficiency: StAR has severe adrenal insufficiency + all steroids low.
Q: Turner syndrome newborn: cardiac, renal, audiologic, ophthalmologic screening — ทำเมื่อไหร่?

ตอบ: Cardiac: echocardiogram at diagnosis — bicuspid aortic valve, coarctation, anomalous pulmonary veins. Renal: renal ultrasound — horseshoe kidney. Audiologic: formal audiologic evaluation (sensorineural hearing loss). Ophthalmologic: at 1 year of age (strabismus risk). Hip dysplasia screening. Endocrine long-term follow-up: short stature, premature ovarian failure (can occur before birth), yearly thyroid function tests (increased hypothyroidism risk). Normal intelligence but decreased visual-spatial skills (Hong 2009).
Q: hCG stimulation test: interpretation of results. Normal response? 5α-reductase? Biosynthetic defect?

ตอบ: Normal response: testosterone > 200 ng/dL (rules out testosterone biosynthetic defect). T:DHT ratio < 8:1 (rules out 5α-reductase deficiency; caveat: T must be adequately elevated to test enzyme function). 5α-reductase deficiency: T normal/elevated, DHT very low, ratio > 8:1. Testosterone biosynthetic defect: T < 200 ng/dL post-hCG. If LH already elevated → hCG not useful (LH and hCG bind same receptor). Gold standard for 5α-reductase: SRD5A2 genetic testing, not T:DHT ratio alone.
Day 86 Preview

Avery Chapter 86 — Disorders of the Thyroid Gland
พรุ่งนี้: Disorders of the Thyroid Gland — หลังเรียน DSD (Chapter 85) เราจะขยับไปอีกหนึ่งในกลุ่ม endocrine disorders. เนื้อหาหลัก: congenital hypothyroidism (most common preventable cause of intellectual disability), newborn screening (NBS) protocol, TSH cutoffs, L-thyroxine dosing, Graves' disease in pregnancy → neonatal hyperthyroidism, thyroid dysgenesis vs dyshormonogenesis, สัมพันธ์กับ Ch84 (Adrenal) และ Ch85 (DSD) ใน Part XVII Endocrine Disorders.
